translations.idiomatic: The king of Chu asked Zhao Xixu how to reply.

sentences.zh: 對曰：『此欲觀吾國之得失而圖之，寶器在於賢臣。

translations.literal: He answered, "This is because they wish to observe our state's gains and losses and plot accordingly; the precious vessels lie in worthy ministers.
translations.idiomatic: Zhao Xixu answered that real treasure was wise ministers, not jade.

sentences.zh: 』遂使恤應之。

translations.literal: " Thereupon he had Xu respond.
translations.idiomatic: The king had him receive the envoy.

sentences.zh: 乃為東面之□四，為南面之□一，為西面之□一。

translations.literal: He then set up four seats on the east side, one seat on the south, one on the west.
translations.idiomatic: He arranged seats for the Qin envoy and Chu ministers.

sentences.zh: 秦使者至，恤曰：『君，客也，請就上位東面，子西南面，太宰子方次之，葉公子高次之，司馬子反次之。

translations.literal: When the Qin envoy arrived, Xu said, "Sir, you are a guest; please take the upper seat on the east; my son on the southwest; Grand Steward Zifang next; Lord Ye Gao next; Sima Zifan next.
translations.idiomatic: Zhao Xixu seated the envoy in the guest of honor place and ranked Chu ministers around him.

sentences.zh: 』恤自居西面之□，稱曰：『客觀楚國之寶器。

translations.literal: " Xu himself took the west seat, declaring, "The guest observes Chu's precious vessels.
translations.idiomatic: Taking the lowest seat himself, he announced that the envoy would see Chu's true treasures.

sentences.zh: 所寶者，賢臣也。

translations.literal: What we treasure are worthy ministers.
translations.idiomatic: Those treasures were living ministers.

sentences.zh: 理百姓，實倉廩，使人各得其所，子西在此。

translations.literal: Who order the hundred surnames, fill granaries, and let each obtain his place—Zixi is here.
translations.idiomatic: Zixi governs the people and fills the granaries.

sentences.zh: 奉珪璋，使諸侯，解忿悁之難，交兩國之歡，使無兵革之憂，太宰子方在此。

translations.literal: Who hold jade tablets and visit the feudal lords, untying the knots of resentment and linking the joy of two states so there is no worry of arms—Grand Steward Zifang is here.
translations.idiomatic: The grand steward settles quarrels between states.

sentences.zh: 守封□，謹境界，不侵鄰國，鄰亦不侵，葉公子高在此。

translations.literal: Who guard the fiefs, are strict about boundaries, do not encroach on neighboring states nor are encroached upon—Lord Ye Gao is here.
translations.idiomatic: Lord Ye Gao guards the borders without provocation.

sentences.zh: 理師旅，正兵戎，以當強敵，提袍鼓以動百萬之觿，使皆赴湯火，蹈白刃，出萬死不顧，司馬子反在此。

translations.literal: Who order the army, correct weapons and armor, to meet strong foes, raise drum and cloak to move a million hosts, making them all rush into boiling water and tread white blades, emerge from ten thousand deaths without looking back—Sima Zifan is here.
translations.idiomatic: Sima Zifan commands armies that will face any peril.

sentences.zh: 若懷霸王之餘義，獵理亂之遺風，昭奚恤在此。

translations.literal: If you embrace the leftover righteousness of hegemons and kings, hunt the surviving wind of ordering chaos—Zhao Xixu is here.
translations.idiomatic: As for the legacy of true kingship, Zhao Xixu himself embodies it.

sentences.zh: 惟大國所觀。

translations.literal: Let the great state observe what it will.
translations.idiomatic: Let your great state look as it pleases.

sentences.zh: 』秦使者瞿然無以對，恤遂攝衣而去。

translations.literal: " The Qin envoy stared blankly, unable to answer; Xu thereupon gathered his robes and departed.
translations.idiomatic: The envoy had no reply; Zhao Xixu rose and left.

sentences.zh: 使反，言秦君曰：『楚多賢臣，未可謀也。

translations.literal: The envoy returned and told the lord of Qin, "Chu has many worthy ministers; it cannot be plotted against.
translations.idiomatic: He reported to Qin that Chu was not to be trifled with.

sentences.zh: 』」注[二]瑋猶美也。

translations.literal: " Comment [2]: wei means to prize or beautify.
translations.idiomatic: Note [2]: wei means to admire or boast of.

sentences.zh: 史記曰，魏惠王問齊威王曰：「王亦有寶乎？

translations.literal: The Records of the Historian says that King Hui of Wei asked King Wei of Qi, "Does your majesty also have treasures?"
translations.idiomatic: The Shiji relates King Hui of Wei asking King Wei of Qi about treasures.

sentences.zh: 」威王曰：「無有。

translations.literal: King Wei said, "I have none."
translations.idiomatic: King Wei answered that he had none.

sentences.zh: 」魏王曰：「寡人之國雖小，尚有徑寸珠照車前後十二乘者十枚，柰何以萬乘之國而無寶乎！

translations.literal: The king of Wei said, "My state, though small, still has ten pearls an inch across that light carriages before and behind—twelve teams; how can a state of ten thousand chariots lack treasures!"
translations.idiomatic: King Hui boasted of ten carriage-lighting pearls.

sentences.zh: 」威王曰：「寡人所以為寶者與王異。

translations.literal: King Wei said, "What I take as treasure differs from your majesty.
translations.idiomatic: King Wei replied that his idea of treasure differed.

sentences.zh: 吾臣有檀子者，使守南城，楚人不敢為寇。

translations.literal: My minister Tanzi, sent to guard the southern city, so that Chu men dare not raid;
translations.idiomatic: His minister Tanzi held the south so Chu dared not raid.

sentences.zh: 吾臣有盼子者，使守高堂，則趙人不敢東漁於河。

translations.literal: my minister Panzi, sent to guard Gaotang, so that Zhao men dare not fish eastward in the river;
translations.idiomatic: Panzi guarded Gaotang so Zhao fishermen stayed west of the river.

sentences.zh: 吾臣有黔夫者，使守徐州，於是燕人祭北門，趙人祭西門，從者七千餘家。

translations.literal: my minister Qianfu, sent to guard Xuzhou, whereupon Yan men sacrificed at the north gate and Zhao men at the west gate, with more than seven thousand households following;
translations.idiomatic: Qianfu drew seven thousand households to Xuzhou from Yan and Zhao.

sentences.zh: 吾臣有種首者，使備盜賊，則道不拾遺。

translations.literal: my minister Shongshou, sent to guard against bandits, so that nothing picked up on the road was kept;
translations.idiomatic: Shongshou kept the roads so honest that lost goods were returned.

sentences.zh: 以此為寶，將以照千里，豈直十二乘哉？

translations.literal: I take these as treasures; they will illumine a thousand li—how are they only twelve teams?
translations.idiomatic: Those, he said, lit a thousand leagues, not a dozen chariots.

sentences.zh: 」魏王籩，不懌而去。

translations.literal: The king of Wei was abashed and departed displeased.
translations.idiomatic: King Hui of Wei slunk away embarrassed.

sentences.zh: 注[三]肆，陳也。

translations.literal: Comment [3]: si means to set forth.
translations.idiomatic: Note [3]: si means to lay out or apply in full.

sentences.zh: 注[四]紀太子僕殺紀公，以其寶玉來奔，納諸宣公，公命與之邑，季文子使司寇出之境。

translations.literal: Comment [4]: the heir-apparent's steward of Ji killed the duke of Ji and fled with his precious jades to Duke Xuan of Lu, who ordered a town given him; Ji Wenzi had the director of crimes expel him beyond the border.
translations.idiomatic: Note [4]: the steward of Ji murdered his lord and fled to Lu with stolen jade; Ji Wenzi had him driven out.

sentences.zh: 公問其故，對曰：「孝敬忠信為吉德，盜賊藏奸為凶德。

translations.literal: The duke asked the reason; he answered, "Filial piety, respect, loyalty, and trust are auspicious virtue; theft, banditry, treachery, and villainy are baleful virtue.
translations.idiomatic: Ji Wenzi explained that receiving a regicide's bribes was baleful virtue.

sentences.zh: 夫莒僕，則其孝敬，*[則]*弒君父矣，則其忠信，則竊寶玉矣，其人則盜賊也，是以去之。

translations.literal: As for the steward of Ju—if we look at his filial respect, he murdered lord and father; if we look at his loyalty and trust, he stole precious jades; as a man he is a bandit and thief—therefore he was cast out.
translations.idiomatic: The steward of Ju combined parricide with theft and had to be banished.

sentences.zh: 舜舉十六相，去四凶，有大功二十而為天子。

translations.literal: Shun raised the sixteen assistants and removed the four villains; he had twenty great merits and became Son of Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: Shun's twenty great deeds included elevating good men and banishing the Four Fiends.

sentences.zh: 今行父雖未獲一吉人，去一凶矣，於舜之功，二十之一也。

translations.literal: Now though Hangfu has not yet obtained one good man, he has removed one villain—for Shun's merit it is one in twenty.
translations.idiomatic: Expelling the steward of Ju was one part in twenty of Shun's achievement.

sentences.zh: 」見左傳。

translations.literal: " See the Zuo Commentary.
translations.idiomatic: The story appears in the Zuo Commentary.

sentences.zh: 注[五]景帝時，韓安國為梁大夫，坐法抵罪。

translations.literal: Comment [5]: in Emperor Jing's time Han Anguo was a grandee of Liang; sitting on a legal offense he was liable to punishment.
translations.idiomatic: Note [5]: Han Anguo of Liang was convicted under Emperor Jing.

sentences.zh: 後梁內史缺，起徒中為二千石，拜為內史。

translations.literal: Later the inner secretary of Liang was vacant; he was raised from convicts to two thousand piculs and appointed inner secretary.
translations.idiomatic: He was later pulled from the convict ranks to serve as inner secretary at two thousand piculs.

sentences.zh: 臣賢案：此言武帝，誤也。

translations.literal: Your servant Xian notes: this saying Emperor Wu is mistaken.
translations.idiomatic: Li Xian corrects the text: it was Jingdi, not Wudi.

sentences.zh: 注[六]張敞為京兆尹，坐殺人亡命歸家。

translations.literal: Comment [6]: Zhang Chang was intendant of the capital; sitting on killing a man he fled his life and returned home.
translations.idiomatic: Note [6]: Zhang Chang fled after a homicide conviction as governor of the capital.

sentences.zh: 冀州亂，征敞為冀州刺史。

translations.literal: When Ji Province was in turmoil, Zhang Chang was summoned as inspector of Ji Province.
translations.idiomatic: During Ji Province's unrest Xuandi summoned him as inspector.

sentences.zh: 注[七]詩小雅曰：「顯允方叔，征伐玁狁，蠻荊來威。

translations.literal: Comment [7]: the ode Xiao ya says, "Bright and true is Fang Shu; he campaigns against the Xianyun; the Man of Jing come in awe."
translations.idiomatic: Note [7]: the ode praises Fang Shu's aweing of the southern Man.

sentences.zh: 」鄭玄注云：「方叔先與吉甫征伐玁狁，今特往伐蠻荊，皆使來服於宣王之威，美其功之多也。

translations.literal: Zheng Xuan's commentary says, "Fang Shu had earlier with Jifu campaigned against the Xianyun; now he goes especially to attack the Man of Jing, both making them come to submit to King Xuan's awe—praising the abundance of his merit."
translations.idiomatic: Zheng Xuan notes Fang Shu's earlier northern campaign and this southern mission.

sentences.zh: 」緄以順帝時討長沙武陵蠻夷有功，故以比之。

translations.literal: " Gun in Shundi's time campaigned against the barbarians of Changsha and Wuling with merit, therefore he is compared.
translations.idiomatic: Feng Gun's southern campaign under Shundi invites the same comparison.

sentences.zh: 注[八]謂祐奏梁冀弟旻，又為司隸校尉，權豪畏之也。

translations.literal: Comment [8]: it means that You memorialized against Liang Ji's younger brother Min, and again as metropolitan commandant the powerful and noble feared him.
translations.idiomatic: Note [8]: Liu You had impeached Liang Min and cowed the mighty as metropolitan commandant.

sentences.zh: 詩曰：「唯仲山甫，柔亦不茹，剛亦不吐，不侮□寡，不畏強禦。」

translations.literal: The Odes says, "Only Zhong Shanfu—soft he will not swallow, hard he will not spit out; he does not insult the widow alone, he does not fear the powerful."
translations.idiomatic: He quoted the ode on Zhong Shanfu's refusal to truckle to the strong or trample the weak.

sentences.zh: 注[九]易解卦象詞也。

translations.literal: Comment [9]: these are the image words of the hexagram Jie in the Changes.
translations.idiomatic: Note [9]: the quotation is the Image text of the hexagram Jie.

sentences.zh: 卦坎下震上。

translations.literal: The hexagram is Kan below and Zhen above.
translations.idiomatic: The hexagram stacks Kan beneath Zhen.

sentences.zh: 解，坎為險，為水。

translations.literal: In Jie, Kan means peril and means water.
translations.idiomatic: In Jie the lower trigram Kan stands for danger and for water.

sentences.zh: 水者，雨之象。

translations.literal: Water is the image of rain.
translations.idiomatic: In this image, water stands for rainfall.

sentences.zh: 震為動，為雷。

translations.literal: Zhen means movement, means thunder.
translations.idiomatic: The Zhen trigram is motion and thunder.

sentences.zh: 王弼注云：「屯難盤結，於是乎解也。」

translations.literal: Wang Bi's commentary says, "Hardship tangled and knotted—thereupon it is released."
translations.idiomatic: Wang Bi reads the hexagram as the point when stubborn troubles finally come undone.

sentences.zh: 再遷，復拜司隸校尉。

translations.literal: Twice promoted, he was again appointed metropolitan commandant.
translations.idiomatic: After further promotion he resumed the metropolitan commandantcy.

sentences.zh: 時張讓弟朔為野王令，貪殘無道，至乃殺孕婦，聞膺厲威嚴，懼罪逃還京師，因匿兄讓弟捨，藏於合柱中。

translations.literal: At the time Zhang Rang's younger brother Shuo was magistrate of Yewang, greedy and cruel beyond the Way, to the point of killing a pregnant woman; hearing of Ying's stern awe, he feared punishment and fled back to the capital, hiding in elder brother Rang's quarters, concealed inside a hollowed pillar.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Shuo, Zhang Rang's brother and magistrate of Yewang, had murdered a pregnant woman; when he heard Li Ying was coming, he fled to Luoyang and hid inside a column in his brother's house.

sentences.zh: 膺知其狀，率將吏卒破柱取朔，付洛陽獄。

translations.literal: Ying knew the circumstances, led officers and soldiers, broke the pillar, took Shuo, and delivered him to the Luoyang jail.
translations.idiomatic: Li Ying learned of it, smashed the pillar, dragged Zhang Shuo out, and threw him into the Luoyang prison.

sentences.zh: 受辭畢，即殺之。

translations.literal: When confession was finished, he immediately executed him.
translations.idiomatic: As soon as the interrogation ended, he had him put to death.

sentences.zh: 讓訴冤於帝，詔膺入殿，御親臨軒，詰以不先請便加誅辟之意。

translations.literal: Rang complained of injustice to the emperor; an edict summoned Ying into the hall; the emperor in person came to the balustrade and questioned him on the idea of not first requesting permission yet applying execution.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Rang appealed to the throne; Emperor Ling summoned Li Ying to the palace and demanded why he had executed a man without prior imperial approval.

sentences.zh: 膺對曰：「昔晉文公執□成公歸於京師，春秋是焉。

translations.literal: Ying replied, "Formerly Duke Wen of Jin seized [Duke Cheng of Wei] and brought him to the capital; the Spring and Autumn approves this.
translations.idiomatic: Li Ying cited Duke Wen of Jin arresting a feudal lord and bringing him to the capital, an act the Spring and Autumn endorsed.

sentences.zh: [一] 禮雲公族有罪，雖曰宥之，有司執憲不從。

translations.literal: [1] The Rites says that when a member of the ducal kin commits a crime, though it is said to pardon him, the officer holding the statute does not obey.
translations.idiomatic: [1] The classics say that even royal relatives who break the law must answer to the judge.

sentences.zh: [二]昔仲尼為魯司寇，七日而誅少正卯。

translations.literal: [2] Formerly Zhong Ni as minister of justice of Lu within seven days executed Shaozheng Mao.
translations.idiomatic: [2] Confucius as Lu's justice minister had Shaozheng Mao killed within a week.

sentences.zh: 今臣到官已積一旬，私懼以稽留為愆，不意獲速疾之罪。

translations.literal: Now your servant has reached office already piled up ten days; privately I feared that delay would be fault; I did not expect to incur the crime of excessive haste.
translations.idiomatic: I have been in post only ten days; I feared delay would be negligence, not that speed would be faulted.

sentences.zh: 誠自知釁責，死不旋踵，特乞留五日，克殄元惡，退就鼎鑊，始生之願也。

translations.literal: I truly know myself guilty and responsible; death does not turn the heel—but I beg to be left five days to destroy the arch-villain, then retire to the cauldron and axe; that is the wish of one newly born.
translations.idiomatic: I accept death; grant me five more days to extirpate the chief criminal, then send me to the executioner.

sentences.zh: 」帝無復言，顧謂讓曰：「此汝弟之罪，司隸何愆？

translations.literal: " The emperor spoke no more, turned to Rang and said, "This is your younger brother's crime—what fault has the metropolitan commandant?
translations.idiomatic: The emperor fell silent, then told Zhang Rang the fault lay with his brother, not Li Ying.

sentences.zh: 」乃遣出之。

translations.literal: " Thereupon he sent him out.
translations.idiomatic: He dismissed Li Ying from the audience.

sentences.zh: 自此諸黃門常侍皆鞠躬屏氣，休沐不敢復出宮省。

translations.literal: From this time the various Yellow Gate regular attendants all bowed the body and held their breath; on rest days they did not dare again leave the palace offices.
translations.idiomatic: After that the eunuchs walked on tiptoe and on their days off dared not leave the palace.

sentences.zh: 帝怪問其故，並叩頭泣曰：「畏李校尉。

translations.literal: The emperor wondered and asked the reason; all knocked their heads weeping, saying, "We fear Colonel Li."
translations.idiomatic: When the emperor asked why, they wept that they feared Metropolitan Commandant Li.

sentences.zh: 」 注[一]公羊傳曰：「晉人執□侯，歸之於京師。

translations.literal: " Comment [1]: the Gongyang Commentary says, "The men of Jin seized [a marquis of Wei] and brought him to the capital.
translations.idiomatic: Note [1]: the Gongyang cites Jin bringing a captive lord to the capital.

sentences.zh: 歸之於者，執之乎天子之側者也。

translations.literal: "To bring him to" means seizing him beside the Son of Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: The formula means the arrest was carried out in the royal presence.

sentences.zh: 罪定不定已可知矣。

translations.literal: Whether guilt is fixed or not can already be known.
translations.idiomatic: Guilt was already plain.

sentences.zh: 」何休注云：「歸之於者，決辭也。」

translations.literal: " He Xiu's commentary says, "To bring him to is a decisive phrase.
translations.idiomatic: He Xiu calls the wording a verdict of condemnation.

sentences.zh: 注[二]解見張酺傳。

translations.literal: Comment [2]: the explanation is seen in Zhang Pu's biography.
translations.idiomatic: Note [2]: see the biography of Zhang Pu for the gloss.

sentences.zh: 是時朝庭日亂，綱紀頹阤，膺獨持風裁，以聲名自高。

translations.literal: At this time the court daily fell into disorder; cords and pattern collapsed; Ying alone upheld standards and judgment, taking high repute from his fame.
translations.idiomatic: While the court slid into chaos, Li Ying alone enforced discipline and let his reputation rise.

sentences.zh: [一]士有被其容接者，名為登龍門。

translations.literal: [1] Gentlemen whom he admitted to his presence were said to have climbed the Dragon Gate.
translations.idiomatic: [1] A private audience with him was called mounting the Dragon Gate.

sentences.zh: [二]及遭黨事，當考實膺等。

translations.literal: [2] When they encountered the partisan affair, they were to examine the facts regarding Ying and others.
translations.idiomatic: [2] When the partisan inquisition began, the court moved to verify charges against Li Ying and his circle.

sentences.zh: 案經三府，太尉陳蕃卻之。

translations.literal: The case passed through the three offices; Grand Commandant Chen Fan rejected it.
translations.idiomatic: The dossier went to the three high ministries; Grand Commandant Chen Fan refused to sign.

sentences.zh: 曰：「今所考案，皆海內人譽，憂國忠公之臣。

translations.literal: He said, "Those now under examination are all men of reputation within the seas, ministers who grieve for the state and serve the public with loyalty.
translations.idiomatic: Chen Fan said the accused were the realm's most honored servants of the public good.

sentences.zh: 此等猶將十世宥也，[三]豈有罪名不章而致收掠者乎？

translations.literal: Such men are still to be pardoned for ten generations, [3] how can there be those whose crime names are not clear yet who are seized and tortured?
translations.idiomatic: [3] Men worthy of ten generations of amnesty should not be jailed on vague charges.

sentences.zh: 」不肯平署。

translations.literal: " He would not jointly sign.
translations.idiomatic: He withheld his countersignature.

sentences.zh: [四]帝愈怒，遂下膺等於黃門北寺獄。

translations.literal: [4] The emperor grew still angrier and thereupon cast Ying and others into the Yellow Gate North Prison.
translations.idiomatic: [4] The emperor, enraged, sent Li Ying and the rest to the eunuch-run North Prison.

sentences.zh: [五]膺等頗引宦官子弟，宦官多懼，請帝以天時宜赦，於是大赦天下。

translations.literal: [5] Ying and others largely implicated the sons and younger brothers of eunuchs; the eunuchs mostly feared and begged the emperor that by heaven's season it was fitting to grant amnesty; thereupon a great amnesty was proclaimed for all under heaven.
translations.idiomatic: [5] Li Ying's testimony threatened eunuch families until the eunuchs begged for a general amnesty.

sentences.zh: 膺免歸鄉里，居陽城山中，天下士大夫皆高尚其道，而污穢朝廷。

translations.literal: Ying was dismissed to his home village, dwelling in the Yangcheng hills; all gentlemen of the realm held his way lofty and deemed the court foul.
translations.idiomatic: Li Ying retired to Yangcheng while scholars everywhere honored him and despised the court.

sentences.zh: [六] 注[一]裁音才代反。

translations.literal: [6] Comment [1]: for cai the reading is the cai-dai fan cut.
translations.idiomatic: Note [6]: the commentary gives the fanqie reading for cai.

sentences.zh: 注[二]以魚為喻也。

translations.literal: Comment [2]: it takes fish as metaphor.
translations.idiomatic: Note [2]: the Dragon Gate image compares scholars to leaping fish.

sentences.zh: 龍門，河水所下之口，在今絳州龍門縣。

translations.literal: Dragon Gate is the mouth where the Yellow River descends; it is today in Longmen county of Jiang prefecture.
translations.idiomatic: Dragon Gate is the gorge of the Yellow River, modern Longmen in Shanxi.

sentences.zh: 辛氏三秦記曰「河津一名龍門，水險不通，魚□之屬莫能上，江海大魚薄集龍門下數千，不得上，上則為龍」也。

translations.literal: Xin's Record of the Three Qins says, "The river ford is also called Dragon Gate; the waters are perilous and impassable; creatures like fish cannot ascend; great fish of the rivers and seas crowd below Dragon Gate by the thousands; unable to go up, if they go up they become dragons."
translations.idiomatic: The Sanqin ji tells how carp mass below the rapids and those that leap through become dragons.

sentences.zh: 注[三]解見耿弇傳。

translations.literal: Comment [3]: the explanation is seen in Geng Yan's biography.
translations.idiomatic: Note [3]: see Geng Yan's biography for the ten-generation pardon gloss.

sentences.zh: 注[四]平署猶連署也。

translations.literal: Comment [4]: ping shu means jointly to sign.
translations.idiomatic: Note [4]: ping shu means cosigning the warrant.

sentences.zh: 注[五]獄名，解見靈紀也。

translations.literal: Comment [5]: it is a prison name; the explanation is seen in the Annals of Ling.
translations.idiomatic: Note [5]: the North Prison is explained in Emperor Ling's annals.

sentences.zh: 注[六]以朝廷為污穢也。

translations.literal: Comment [6]: it takes the court as foul.
translations.idiomatic: Note [6]: scholars treated the court as corrupt by contrast.

sentences.zh: 及陳蕃免太尉，朝野屬意於膺，荀爽恐其名高致禍，欲令屈節以全亂世，為書貽曰：「久廢過庭，不聞善誘，陟岵瞻望，惟日為歲。

translations.literal: When Chen Fan was removed as grand commandant, court and wilds pinned hope on Ying; Xun Shuang, fearing his lofty name would bring disaster, wished to make him bend his integrity to preserve himself in a chaotic age, and sent a letter saying, "Long unused to passing the courtyard, I have not heard good guidance; climbing the hill-mound I gaze in hope—each day seems a year.
translations.idiomatic: After Chen Fan fell, all eyes turned to Li Ying; Xun Shuang wrote urging him to bend a little to survive the times, opening with courteous regret at long separation.

sentences.zh: [一]知以直道不容於時，悅山樂水，家於陽城。

translations.literal: [1] I know that by the straight Way you are not tolerated in the time; you delight in mountains and rejoice in water, making your home at Yangcheng.
translations.idiomatic: [1] He acknowledged that Li Ying's integrity had blocked his career and praised his mountain retreat.

sentences.zh: 道近路夷，當即聘問，無狀嬰疾，闕於所仰。

translations.literal: The road is near and the way level—I ought at once to pay a visit; without excuse I have fallen ill, failing toward what I look up to.
translations.idiomatic: He apologized for not visiting sooner, blaming illness.

sentences.zh: 頃聞上帝震怒，貶黜鼎臣，[二]人鬼同謀，[三]以為天子當貞觀二五，利見大人，[四]不謂夷之初旦，明而未融，[五]虹蜺揚輝，□和取同。

translations.literal: Recently I heard that the High Lord was shaken with wrath and degraded the tripod minister, [2] men and ghosts sharing counsel, [3] thinking the Son of Heaven ought to hold firm the view of the second and fifth lines and see the great man, [4] not expecting the wounding at the first dawn of Yi, bright yet not clear, [5] rainbow and secondary rainbow raising radiance, [lacuna] harmony taking sameness.
translations.idiomatic: He alluded in dense classical imagery to Chen Fan's dismissal, ill omens, and a darkened court.

sentences.zh: [六]方今天地氣閉，大人休否，[七]智者見險，投以遠害。

translations.literal: [6] Now heaven and earth's breath is closed; the great man rests in obstruction, [7] the wise see peril and cast themselves far from harm.
translations.idiomatic: [6-7] He cited hexagrams of closure and obstruction and urged the wise to flee danger.

sentences.zh: [八]雖匱人望，內合私願。

translations.literal: [8] Though it fails human expectation, inwardly it matches private wish.
translations.idiomatic: [8] He admitted that retreat disappointed the world yet suited Li Ying's private peace.

sentences.zh: [九]想甚欣然，不為恨也。

translations.literal: [9] I imagine you are quite content and harbor no resentment.
translations.idiomatic: [9] He hoped Li Ying bore no grudge for the advice.

sentences.zh: 願怡神無事，偃息衡門，[一０] 任其飛沉，與時抑揚。

translations.literal: I beg you to soothe the spirit in quiet, rest at the crossbeam gate, [10] letting things float or sink, bending and rising with the times.
translations.idiomatic: He urged calm seclusion at a humble gate, rising and falling with fortune.

sentences.zh: 」頃之，帝崩。

translations.literal: " Before long the emperor died.
translations.idiomatic: Soon afterward Emperor Huan died.

sentences.zh: 陳蕃為太傅，與大將軍竇武共秉朝政，連謀誅諸宦官，故引用天下名士，乃以膺為長樂少府。

translations.literal: Chen Fan became grand tutor and with great general Dou Wu together held court government; plotting continuously to execute the various eunuchs, they therefore drew in famous gentlemen of the realm, and appointed Ying chamberlain for the palace of Changlo.
translations.idiomatic: Chen Fan and Dou Wu took power, plotted against the eunuchs, and recalled Li Ying as chamberlain of Changlo.

sentences.zh: 及陳、竇之敗，膺等復廢。

translations.literal: When Chen and Dou failed, Ying and others were again cast aside.
translations.idiomatic: After Chen Fan and Dou Wu fell, Li Ying lost office again.

sentences.zh: 注[一]論語曰：「鯉趨而過庭。

translations.literal: Comment [1]: the Analects says, "The carp hurried across the courtyard.
translations.idiomatic: Note [1]: Xun Shuang alludes to Confucius teaching his son in the courtyard.

sentences.zh: 子曰：『學詩乎？

translations.literal: The Master said, Have you studied the Odes?
translations.idiomatic: Confucius asks whether the boy has studied the Odes.

sentences.zh: 』曰『未也』。

translations.literal: He said, Not yet.
translations.idiomatic: The boy answers not yet.

sentences.zh: 」又曰：「孔子恂恂然善誘人。

translations.literal: " It also says, "Confucius was gentle and skillful in leading others on.
translations.idiomatic: He cites Confucius as a patient teacher.

sentences.zh: 」詩曰：「陟彼岵兮，瞻望父兮。

translations.literal: " The Odes says, "I climb that hill-mound, gazing toward my father.
translations.idiomatic: He quotes the ode on longing for a father figure.

sentences.zh: 」又曰：「一日不見，如三歲兮。

translations.literal: " It also says, "One day not seen is like three years.
translations.idiomatic: He adds the ode on aching absence.

sentences.zh: 」爽致敬於膺，故以父為喻也。

translations.literal: " Shuang showed respect to Ying; therefore he used the father as metaphor.
translations.idiomatic: Xun Shuang styled Li Ying a father-teacher in classical idiom.

sentences.zh: 注[二]上帝謂天子，鼎臣即陳蕃。

translations.literal: Comment [2]: High Lord means the Son of Heaven; the tripod minister is Chen Fan.
translations.idiomatic: Note [2]: High Lord is the emperor; the tripod minister is Chen Fan.

sentences.zh: 注[三]易下系曰：「人謀鬼謀，百姓與能。」

translations.literal: Comment [3]: the Changes lower appendix says, "Human counsel, ghost counsel—the hundred clans share the ability."
translations.idiomatic: Note [3]: he quotes the Changes on human and spirit counsel agreeing.

sentences.zh: 注[四]易曰：「天地之道，貞觀也。

translations.literal: Comment [4]: the Changes says, "The Way of heaven and earth is to hold firm the view."
translations.idiomatic: Note [4]: he cites the Changes on the steadfast vision of heaven and earth.

sentences.zh: 」干九二、九五並曰「利見大人」也。

translations.literal: " On the second and fifth lines of Qian both say, "It is favorable to see the great man.
translations.idiomatic: He adds the Qian hexagram lines on seeing the great man.

sentences.zh: 注[五]夷，傷也。

translations.literal: Comment [5]: yi means wounding.
translations.idiomatic: Note [5]: yi in Ming yi means wounding.

sentences.zh: 融，朗也。

translations.literal: Rong means clear.
translations.idiomatic: Rong means clarity or brightness.

sentences.zh: 明夷卦離下坤上，離為日，坤為地，日之初出，其明未朗。

translations.literal: The hexagram Ming yi is Li below Kun above; Li is the sun, Kun is earth; at the sun's first emergence its clarity is not yet clear.
translations.idiomatic: He explains the Ming yi image as the dim first light of dawn.

sentences.zh: 左傳曰：「明而未融，其當旦乎？

translations.literal: The Zuo Commentary says, "Bright yet not clear—should this be dawn?"
translations.idiomatic: He quotes the Zuo on twilight brightness.

sentences.zh: 」以膺黜，故喻之也。

translations.literal: " Because Ying was degraded, therefore it is likened.
translations.idiomatic: He applies the image to Li Ying's fall from office.

sentences.zh: 注[六]春秋考異郵曰：「虹蜺出，亂惑□和。

translations.literal: Comment [6]: the Spring and Autumn Kaoyi you says, "When rainbow and secondary rainbow appear, confusion disorders [lacuna] harmony."
translations.idiomatic: Note [6]: an omen text links double rainbows with a corrupted harmony.

sentences.zh: 」謂□君子，同小人也。

translations.literal: " It means [lacuna] the gentleman, taking sameness with petty men.
translations.idiomatic: It warns against gentlemen allying with petty men.

sentences.zh: 論語曰：

translations.literal: The Analects says,
translations.idiomatic: He turns to the Analects.

sentences.zh: 「君子和而不同，小人同而不和」也。

translations.literal: "The gentleman harmonizes but does not merely agree; the petty man agrees but does not harmonize."
translations.idiomatic: He quotes the distinction between harmony and mere conformity.

sentences.zh: 注[七]易文言曰：「天地閉，賢人隱。

translations.literal: Comment [7]: the Changes Wen yan says, "When heaven and earth are closed, worthy men hide."
translations.idiomatic: Note [7]: the Wen yan counsels reclusion when heaven and earth close.

sentences.zh: 」否九五曰：「大人休否。

translations.literal: " On the fifth line of Pi it says, "The great man rests from obstruction.
translations.idiomatic: He cites Pi's fifth line on the great man pausing in obstruction.

sentences.zh: 」休否謂休廢而否塞。

translations.literal: " Resting from obstruction means resting in dismissal while obstruction blocks."
translations.idiomatic: He glosses resting from obstruction as withdrawal while the age stays blocked.

sentences.zh: 注[八]見險難，故投身以遠害也。

translations.literal: Comment [8]: seeing peril and difficulty, therefore he casts the body to flee harm.
translations.idiomatic: Note [8]: the wise withdraw to escape danger.

sentences.zh: 易曰：「君子以儉德避難，不可榮以祿。」

translations.literal: The Changes says, "The gentleman by frugal virtue flees difficulty; he cannot be honored with salary."
translations.idiomatic: He quotes the gentleman's duty to refuse salary and hide.

sentences.zh: 注[九]匱，乏也。

translations.literal: Comment [9]: kui means lacking.
translations.idiomatic: Note [9]: kui means to fall short of expectation.

sentences.zh: 注[一０]毛萇詩注曰：「衡門，橫木為門。」

translations.literal: Comment [10]: Mao Chang's commentary on the Odes says, "The crossbeam gate is a gate of horizontal timber."
translations.idiomatic: Note [10]: the humble crossbeam gate marks a scholar's poor dwelling.

sentences.zh: 後張儉事起，收捕鉤黨，鄉人謂膺曰：「可去矣。

translations.literal: Later when Zhang Jian's affair arose, arrests hooked the partisans; his townsman said to Ying, "You may go."
translations.idiomatic: When Zhang Jian's case triggered arrests, a neighbor urged Li Ying to flee.

sentences.zh: 」對曰：「事不辭難，罪不逃刑，臣之節也。

translations.literal: He answered, "In affairs not to shun difficulty, in guilt not to flee punishment—that is a minister's integrity.
translations.idiomatic: Li Ying answered that a minister does not dodge hardship or punishment.

sentences.zh: [一]吾年已六十，死生有命，去將安之？

translations.literal: [1] My years are already sixty; life and death have their mandate—where would I go if I left?
translations.idiomatic: [1] At sixty he asked where exile could lead but disgrace.

sentences.zh: 」乃詣詔獄。

translations.literal: " Thereupon he went to the edict prison.
translations.idiomatic: He walked to the imperial prison and surrendered.

sentences.zh: 考死，妻子徙邊，門生、故吏及其父兄，並被禁錮。

translations.literal: He was examined to death; wife and children were banished to the frontier; disciples, former subordinates, and their fathers and elder brothers were all proscribed.
translations.idiomatic: He died under torture; his family was exiled and his followers barred from office.

sentences.zh: 注[一]左傳曰，晉侯之弟楊干亂行於曲梁，魏絳戮其僕。

translations.literal: Comment [1]: the Zuo Commentary says that the younger brother of the marquis of Jin, Yang Gan, disordered the ranks at Quiliang, and Wei Jiang executed his charioteer.
translations.idiomatic: Note [1]: the gloss cites Wei Jiang executing a noble's attendant despite danger.

sentences.zh: 晉侯怒，謂羊舌赤曰：

translations.literal: The marquis of Jin was angry and said to Yangshe Chi,
translations.idiomatic: The duke of Jin fumed to Yangshe Chi.

sentences.zh: 「合諸侯以為榮也。

translations.literal: "To assemble the feudal lords was for glory.
translations.idiomatic: He said the league of states was meant to bring glory.

sentences.zh: 楊干為戮，何辱如之？

translations.literal: Yang Gan was executed—what shame like this?
translations.idiomatic: Executing Yang Gan shamed the host.

sentences.zh: 必殺魏絳，無失也。

translations.literal: You must kill Wei Jiang without fail.
translations.idiomatic: He ordered Wei Jiang killed.

sentences.zh: 」對曰：「絳無貳志，事君不避難，有罪不逃刑，其將來辭，何辱命焉！」

translations.literal: " He answered, "Jiang has no second mind; serving the lord he does not shun difficulty; having guilt he does not flee punishment—he will soon come to explain; why shame the command?
translations.idiomatic: The reply was that Wei Jiang would answer for himself and need not be summoned in disgrace.

sentences.zh: 時侍御史蜀郡景毅子顧為膺門徒，而未有錄牒，故不及於譴。

translations.literal: At the time the attendant secretary Jing Yi of Shu commandery had a son Gu who was Ying's disciple, but there was no registered slip, therefore he did not reach condemnation.
translations.idiomatic: Jing Gu studied under Li Ying but was omitted from the roster and escaped prosecution.

sentences.zh: 毅乃慨然曰：「本謂膺賢，遣子師之，豈可以漏奪名籍，苟安而已！

translations.literal: Yi thereupon sighed and said, "I originally thought Ying worthy and sent my son to study with him—how can I, because the name list was leaked away, rest in mere safety!"
translations.idiomatic: Jing Yi refused to hide behind a clerical error.

sentences.zh: 」遂自表免歸，時人義之。

translations.literal: " Thereupon he submitted a memorial on his own and was dismissed to return home; people of the time deemed him righteous.
translations.idiomatic: He petitioned to be struck from office anyway, and contemporaries honored him.

sentences.zh: 膺子瓚

translations.literal: Ying's son Zan
translations.idiomatic: Li Ying's son Zan

sentences.zh: 膺子瓚，位至東平相。

translations.literal: Ying's son Zan rose to the rank of chancellor of Dongping.
translations.idiomatic: His son Li Zan became chancellor of Dongping.

sentences.zh: [一]初，曹操微時，瓚異其才，將沒，謂子宣等曰：「時將亂矣，天下英雄無過曹操。

translations.literal: [1] At first, when Cao Cao was still obscure, Zan marveled at his talent; about to die, he told sons Xuan and others, saying, "The age will soon be in chaos; among heroes under heaven none surpass Cao Cao.
translations.idiomatic: [1] Li Zan, seeing Cao Cao's genius while he was still unknown, told his sons on his deathbed that Cao would tower over the coming chaos.

sentences.zh: 張孟卓與吾善，袁本初汝外親，雖爾勿依，必歸曹氏。

translations.literal: Zhang Mengzhuo is on good terms with me; Yuan Benchu is your maternal kin—though so, do not rely on them; you must attach yourselves to the Cao clan.
translations.idiomatic: He warned them not to rely even on friends or in-laws but to follow Cao Cao.

sentences.zh: 」諸子從之，並免於亂世。

translations.literal: " The various sons followed this and all escaped harm in the chaotic age.
translations.idiomatic: They followed his advice and survived the wars.

sentences.zh: 注[一]謝承書「瓚」作「珪」。

translations.literal: Comment [1]: Xie Cheng's book writes Zan as gui.
translations.idiomatic: Note [1]: Xie Cheng writes the name as gui instead of zan.

sentences.zh: 杜密字周甫，穎川陽城人也。

translations.literal: Du Mi, courtesy name Zhoufu, was a man of Yangcheng in Yingchuan.
translations.idiomatic: Du Mi, style Zhoufu, came from Yangcheng in Yingchuan.

sentences.zh: 為人沉質，少有厲俗志。

translations.literal: As a person he was deep and plain; in youth he had the ambition to brace custom.
translations.idiomatic: He was grave and steady by nature, and from boyhood meant to stiffen public morals.

sentences.zh: 為司徒胡廣所辟，稍遷代郡太守。

translations.literal: He was summoned by Minister of Education Hu Guang and gradually promoted to governor of Dai.
translations.idiomatic: Hu Guang brought him in, and he rose step by step to governor of Dai.

sentences.zh: 征，三遷太山太守、北海相。

translations.literal: Summoned, thrice promoted to governor of Taishan and chancellor of Beihai.
translations.idiomatic: Recalled to court, he climbed three ranks to governor of Taishan and chancellor of Beihai.

sentences.zh: 其宦官子弟為令長有奸惡者，輒捕案之。

translations.literal: As for eunuchs' sons who were magistrates and had wickedness, he always seized and investigated them.
translations.idiomatic: Whenever a eunuch's son held a county magistracy and turned corrupt, Du Mi had him arrested and charged.

sentences.zh: 行春到高密縣，見鄭玄為鄉佐，知其異器，即召署郡職，遂遣就學。

translations.literal: On his spring inspection he reached Gaomi county, saw Zheng Xuan serving as township assistant, knew he was extraordinary material, at once summoned him to a commandery post, and sent him off to study.
translations.idiomatic: During a spring tour of Gaomi he spotted Zheng Xuan as a minor clerk, recognized his genius, gave him a county appointment, and sponsored his further study.

sentences.zh: 後密去官還家，每謁守令，多所陳托。

translations.literal: Later Mi left office and returned home; whenever he visited guardians and magistrates he made many entreaties on others' behalf.
translations.idiomatic: After Du Mi retired, he still lobbied local officials on every visit.

sentences.zh: 同郡劉勝，亦自蜀郡告歸鄉里，閉門埽軌，無所幹及。

translations.literal: Liu Sheng of the same commandery also had announced his return from Shu commandery to his home village, closing his gate and sweeping the tracks, having nothing to do with outreach.
translations.idiomatic: His townsman Liu Sheng had likewise quit Shu and shut himself away from the world.

sentences.zh: [一]太守王昱謂密曰：「劉季陵清高士，公卿多舉之者。

translations.literal: [1] Governor Wang Yu said to Mi, "Liu Jiling is a lofty pure gentleman; dukes and ministers often recommend him.
translations.idiomatic: [1] Governor Wang Yu told Du Mi that Liu Sheng was a recluse much praised at court.

sentences.zh: 」密知昱激己，對曰：「劉勝位為大夫，見禮上賓，而知善不薦，聞惡無言，隱情惜己，自同寒蟬，此罪人也。

translations.literal: " Mi knew Yu was provoking him and replied, "Liu Sheng held rank as grandee, was received as a state guest, yet when he knew good he did not recommend it, when he heard evil he spoke not—hiding the truth and sparing himself, the same as a winter cicada: that is a criminal.
translations.idiomatic: Du Mi saw the barb and answered that a man who hoards silence while holding office is no better than a mute cicada.

sentences.zh: [二]今志義力行之賢而密達之，[三]違道失節之士而密糾之，使明府賞刑得中，令問休揚，不亦萬分之一乎？

translations.literal: [2] Now I report to you, my worthy, men who vigorously practice righteousness, [3] and I investigate those who abandon the Way and lose integrity—so that your excellency's rewards and punishments hit the mark and your fair name spreads—is that not one part in ten thousand?
translations.idiomatic: [2-3] He pledged to forward the diligent and expose the corrupt so the governor's justice would shine.

sentences.zh: 」昱籩服，待之彌厚。

translations.literal: " Yu was ashamed and deferential and treated him all the more generously.
translations.idiomatic: Wang Yu accepted the rebuke and treated Du Mi with greater respect.

sentences.zh: 注[一]軌，車多也。

translations.literal: Comment [1]: gui means the tracks of carriages.
translations.idiomatic: Note [1]: the gloss explains gui as wheel-ruts.

sentences.zh: 言絕人事。

translations.literal: It means cutting off human affairs.
translations.idiomatic: It describes severing worldly ties.

sentences.zh: 注[二]寒蟬謂寂默也。

translations.literal: Comment [2]: "cold cicada" means silent stillness.
translations.idiomatic: Note [2]: the winter cicada stands for cowed silence.

sentences.zh: 楚詞曰：「悲哉秋之為氣也，蟬寂漠而無聲。」

translations.literal: The Songs of Chu says, "Alas for autumn's breath—the cicada is lonely and without sound."
translations.idiomatic: The Chuci likens autumn to a voiceless cicada.

sentences.zh: 注[三]力行謂盡力行善也。

translations.literal: Comment [3]: "vigorous practice" means doing good with full strength.
translations.idiomatic: Note [3]: vigorous practice means pressing every effort toward virtue.

sentences.zh: 禮記曰：「好問近乎智，力行近乎仁。」

translations.literal: The Record of Rites says, "Fondness for asking is close to wisdom; vigorous practice is close to humanity."
translations.idiomatic: The Liji pairs eager inquiry with wisdom and resolute action with benevolence.

sentences.zh: 後桓帝征拜尚書令，遷河南尹，轉太僕。

translations.literal: Later Emperor Huan summoned him minister secretary, promoted him intendant of Henan, transferred him grand coachman.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Huan later named him minister secretary, then governor of Henan, then grand coachman.

sentences.zh: 黨事既起，免歸本郡，與李膺俱坐，而名行相次，故時人亦稱「李杜」焉。

translations.literal: When the partisan affair arose he was dismissed to his native commandery; together with Li Ying he sat under sentence, and because their names and conduct ranked side by side, people of the time also called them "Li and Du."
translations.idiomatic: The proscription caught him with Li Ying, and the pair were nicknamed the second Li and Du.

sentences.zh: [一]後太傅陳蕃輔政，復為太僕。

translations.literal: [1] Later, when Grand Tutor Chen Fan assisted government, he again became grand coachman.
translations.idiomatic: [1] Under Chen Fan he returned as grand coachman.

sentences.zh: 明年，坐黨事被征，自殺。

translations.literal: The next year, sitting on the partisan affair he was summoned and killed himself.
translations.idiomatic: The following year a new warrant for partisans drove him to suicide.

sentences.zh: 注[一]前有李固、杜喬，故言「亦」也。

translations.literal: Comment [1]: before there were Li Gu and Du Qiao; therefore it says "also."
translations.idiomatic: Note [1]: the text says "also" because an earlier famous Li-Du pair existed.

sentences.zh: 劉祐字伯祖，中山安國人也。

translations.literal: Liu You, courtesy name Bozu, was a man of Anguo in Zhongshan.
translations.idiomatic: Liu You, style Bozu, came from Anguo in Zhongshan.

sentences.zh: [一]安國後別屬博陵。

translations.literal: [1] Anguo later was separately attached to Boling.
translations.idiomatic: [1] Anguo county was later reassigned to Boling.

sentences.zh: 祐初察孝廉，補尚書侍郎，閒練故事，文札強辨，每有奏議，應對無滯，為僚類所歸。

translations.literal: You at first passed the filial-and-incorrupt examination, supplemented as gentleman of the masters of writing, was practiced in precedent, strong in documents and debate; whenever there were memorials and proposals his responses never stuck—he was the one to whom colleagues looked.
translations.idiomatic: Recommended filial and incorrupt, he mastered palace paperwork and became the colleague everyone relied on in debate.

sentences.zh: 注[一]安國，縣，故城在今定州義豐縣東南。

translations.literal: Comment [1]: Anguo is a county; its old walled city lies southeast of Yifeng in Dingzhou today.
translations.idiomatic: Note [1]: old Anguo lay southeast of present Yifeng in Hebei.

sentences.zh: 謝承書曰：「祐，宗室胤緒，代有名位。

translations.literal: Xie Cheng's book says, "You was of imperial-clan descent; generation after generation there were famous positions."
translations.idiomatic: Xie Cheng traces him to Han imperial kin with a long record of office.

sentences.zh: 少修操行，學嚴氏春秋、小戴禮、古文尚書，仕郡為主簿。

translations.literal: In youth he cultivated conduct, studied the Yan family Spring and Autumn, the Lesser Dai Rites, and the old-text Documents, and served the commandery as chief clerk.
translations.idiomatic: He studied the Yan Chunqiu, the Lesser Dai Liji, and the old-text Documents before becoming chief clerk of his commandery.

sentences.zh: 郡將小子嘗出錢付之，今市買果實，祐悉以買筆書具與之，因白郡將，言『郎君年可入小學，而但傲佷，遠近謂明府無過庭之教，請出授書』。

translations.literal: The commandery chief once gave his young son money to hand to You, ordering him to buy fruit in the market; You used it all to buy brushes and writing gear, returned it to the boy, and reported to the chief, saying, "Your young lord is old enough for elementary school, yet he is only arrogant and stubborn; far and near they say your excellency lacks the teaching of the courtyard—please send him out for schooling."
translations.idiomatic: When the governor's son asked him to buy fruit, he spent the money on school supplies and bluntly told the father the boy needed a tutor, not treats.

sentences.zh: 郡將為使子就祐受經，五日一試，不滿呈限，白決罰，遂成學業也。」

translations.literal: " The chief had his son go to You to receive the classics, testing every five days; if he did not meet the stated limit, You reported for punishment—thus the learning was completed."
translations.idiomatic: The governor then placed the boy under Liu You's tutelage with regular examinations until the youth actually learned the texts.

sentences.zh: 除任城令，兗州舉為尤異，遷揚州刺史。

translations.literal: He was appointed magistrate of Rencheng; Yanzhou recommended him as outstanding and he was promoted inspector of Yangzhou.
translations.idiomatic: As magistrate of Rencheng he won an exceptional rating and rose to inspector of Yangzhou.

sentences.zh: 是時會稽太守梁旻，大將軍冀之從弟也。

translations.literal: At that time the governor of Kuaiji, Liang Min, was a cousin of Great General Liang Ji.
translations.idiomatic: His first clash with power came when Kuaiji governor Liang Min, Liang Ji's cousin, broke the law.

sentences.zh: 祐舉奏其罪，旻坐征。

translations.literal: You raised a memorial impeaching his crimes; Min sat under investigation and was summoned.
translations.idiomatic: Liu You impeached Liang Min and had him recalled for trial.

sentences.zh: 復遷祐河東太守。

translations.literal: You was again promoted to governor of Hedong.
translations.idiomatic: He was then promoted to governor of Hedong.

sentences.zh: 時屬縣令長率多中官子弟，百姓患之。

translations.literal: At the time the county magistrates under his rule were for the most part sons of inner-court eunuchs, and the common people suffered from them.
translations.idiomatic: Hedong's counties were stuffed with eunuchs' sons as magistrates, to the people's misery.

sentences.zh: 祐到，黜其權強，平理冤結，政為三河表。

translations.literal: When You arrived he dismissed the powerful, settled tangled wrongs, and his government became the model of the Three Rivers.
translations.idiomatic: He purged the powerful, cleared old injustices, and set the standard for the whole Three Rivers region.

sentences.zh: [一] 注[一]三河謂河東、河內、河南也。

translations.literal: [1] Comment [1]: the Three Rivers means Hedong, Henei, and Henan.
translations.idiomatic: [1] Note [1]: the Three Rivers are Hedong, Henei, and Henan.

sentences.zh: 表猶標準也。

translations.literal: Biao means a measuring line or standard.
translations.idiomatic: Here biao means the yardstick other governors tried to match.

sentences.zh: 再遷，延熹四年，拜尚書令，又出為河南尹，轉司隸校尉。

translations.literal: Twice promoted: in the fourth year of Yanxi he was appointed minister secretary, then went out as intendant of Henan, then transferred metropolitan commandant.
translations.idiomatic: In 161 he became minister secretary, then governor of Henan, then metropolitan commandant.

sentences.zh: 時權貴子弟罷州郡還入京師者，每至界首，輒改易輿服，隱匿財寶，威行朝廷。

translations.literal: At the time when noble sons left their provinces and returned to the capital, each time they reached the border they changed carriage and dress and hid treasures—his awe ran through the court.
translations.idiomatic: So feared was he that magnates' sons shed silks and hid gold before crossing into his jurisdiction.

sentences.zh: 拜宗正，三轉大司農。

translations.literal: He was appointed director of the imperial clan, thrice transferred to minister of finance.
translations.idiomatic: He directed the imperial clan office and thrice rose to minister of finance.

sentences.zh: 時中常侍蘇康、管霸用事於內，遂固天下良田美業，山林湖澤，民庶窮困，州郡累氣。

translations.literal: At the time the regular attendants Su Kang and Guan Ba wielded power within the palace and thereupon monopolized the realm's good fields and fine estates, mountains, forests, lakes, and marshes; the common people were driven to destitution and the commanderies held their breath.
translations.idiomatic: Su Kang and Guan Ba seized prime farmland and waterways until provinces dared not breathe a protest.

sentences.zh: [一]祐移書所在，依科品沒入之。

translations.literal: [1] You sent letters to the places involved and, according to statute and grade, confiscated the properties.
translations.idiomatic: [1] Liu You ordered local officials to confiscate those estates under statute.

sentences.zh: 桓帝大怒，論祐輸左校。

translations.literal: Emperor Huan was greatly angry and sentenced You to labor at the Left Work site.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Huan, furious, sent him to the Left Work gangs.

sentences.zh: 注[一]累氣，屏息也。

translations.literal: Comment [1]: lei qi means holding the breath.
translations.idiomatic: Note [1]: lei qi describes officials too frightened to exhale.

sentences.zh: 後得赦出，復歷三卿，輒以疾辭，乞骸骨歸田裡。

translations.literal: Later he obtained release by amnesty, again passed through the three minister-grade posts, but always declined citing illness and begged to surrender his bones to field and hamlet.
translations.idiomatic: After a pardon he cycled through top ministries yet pleaded illness until he was allowed to retire.

sentences.zh: 詔拜中散大夫，遂杜門絕多。

translations.literal: An edict appointed him chief of staff at court; thereupon he bolted his gate and cut short most visits.
translations.idiomatic: The court named him a chief counselor, whereupon he sealed his gate and turned away casual callers.

sentences.zh: 每三公缺，朝廷皆屬意於祐，以譖毀不用。

translations.literal: Whenever one of the three dukes was vacant the court pinned hope on You, but because of slander he was not used.
translations.idiomatic: Each time a trio seat opened, rumor blocked his appointment.

sentences.zh: 延篤貽之書曰：「昔太伯三讓，人無德而稱焉。

translations.literal: Yan Du sent him a letter saying, "Formerly Taibo yielded thrice, yet people had no special virtue to praise in him.
translations.idiomatic: Yan Du wrote praising Liu You's humility, opening with Taibo's three refusals of the throne.

sentences.zh: [一]延陵高揖，華夏仰風。

translations.literal: [1] Yanling bowed high; the Hua-xia looked to the wind.
translations.idiomatic: [1] He compared him to Prince Jizha of Wu, whose courtesy bow awed the central states.

sentences.zh: [二]吾子懷蘧氏之可卷，體寧子之如愚，[三]微妙玄通，沖而不盈，[四]蔑三光之明，未暇以天下為事，何其劭與！

translations.literal: [2] My lord cherishes what Qu can roll up and embodies Ning's seeming foolishness, [3] subtle, mysterious, and penetrating, empty yet never full, [4] disdaining the brightness of the three luminaries and having no leisure to busy himself with the realm—how admirable!
translations.idiomatic: [2-4] He piled classical compliments on Liu You's Daoist restraint and refusal of high office.

sentences.zh: 」[五] 注[一]三讓，解見和紀。

translations.literal: " [5] Comment [1]: the three yields are explained in the Annals of He."
translations.idiomatic: [5] Note [1]: see the He annals for Taibo's three yields.

sentences.zh: 注[二]揖，讓也。

translations.literal: Comment [2]: yi means to yield.
translations.idiomatic: Note [2]: yi means deferential yielding.

sentences.zh: 左傳，吳王壽夢卒，子諸樊既除喪，將立弟季札，札□其室而耕，乃捨之。

translations.literal: The Zuo Commentary: when King Shoumeng of Wu died, his son Zhufan, having finished mourning, was about to establish his younger brother Jizha; Jizha fled to farm in his chamber, and the plan was dropped.
translations.idiomatic: The gloss recounts Prince Jizha refusing the throne of Wu.

sentences.zh: 注[三]蘧瑗字伯玉，寧子名俞，並□大夫。

translations.literal: Comment [3]: Qu Yuan styled Boyu and Ning Yu are both grandees of Wei.
translations.idiomatic: Note [3]: Qu Boyu and Ning Wuzi were both Wei ministers.

sentences.zh: 論語孔子曰：「君子哉蘧伯玉，邦有道則仕，邦無道則可卷而懷之。

translations.literal: The Analects: Confucius said, "What a gentleman was Qu Boyu! When the state had the Way he served; when it lacked the Way he could roll up his talents and keep them in his breast.
translations.idiomatic: He quoted Confucius on Qu Boyu's ability to withdraw when the age turned dark.

sentences.zh: 」又曰：「寧武子邦無道則愚。」

translations.literal: " It also says, "Ning Wuzi, when the state lacked the Way, was foolish.
translations.idiomatic: He added Confucius on Ning Wuzi's studied simplicity.

sentences.zh: 注[四]老子曰「古之善為道者，微妙玄通，深不可識」也。

translations.literal: Comment [4]: Laozi says, "Those good at the Way in antiquity were subtle, mysterious, and penetrating, too deep to be known."
translations.idiomatic: Note [4]: Laozi describes the sage as subtle and unfathomable.

sentences.zh: 又曰「道沖而用之或不盈」。

translations.literal: It also says, "The Way is empty; yet in using it one is not likely to fill it."
translations.idiomatic: Laozi also calls the Way a vessel that cannot be filled.

sentences.zh: 注[五]莊子曰：「舜讓天下於子州支伯，子州支伯曰：『予適有幽憂之病，方且理之，未暇理天下也。

translations.literal: Comment [5]: Zhuangzi says, "Shun wished to yield the realm to Zizhou Zhibo, who said, I happen to have an illness of dark sorrow; I am still treating it and have no leisure to order the realm.
translations.idiomatic: Note [5]: Zhuangzi tells how a recluse refused Shun's offer of the throne.

sentences.zh: 』」靈帝初，陳蕃輔政，以祐為河南尹。

translations.literal: " At the beginning of Emperor Ling's reign, when Chen Fan assisted government, he appointed You intendant of Henan.
translations.idiomatic: Early in Emperor Ling's reign Chen Fan made him governor of Henan again.

sentences.zh: 及蕃敗，祐黜歸，卒於家。

translations.literal: When Fan fell, You was dismissed and returned, and died at home.
translations.idiomatic: When Chen Fan fell, Liu You went home and died there.

sentences.zh: 明年，大誅黨人，幸不及禍。

translations.literal: The next year came the great slaughter of partisans; by good fortune he did not reach that disaster.
translations.idiomatic: The next year's partisan bloodbath missed him by luck.

sentences.zh: 魏朗字少英，會稽上虞人也。

translations.literal: Wei Lang, courtesy name Shaoying, was a man of Shangyu in Kuaiji.
translations.idiomatic: Wei Lang, style Shaoying, came from Shangyu in Kuaiji.

sentences.zh: [一]少為縣吏。

translations.literal: [1] In youth he was a county clerk.
translations.idiomatic: [1] He began as a petty county clerk.

sentences.zh: 兄為鄉人所殺，朗白日操刃報讎於縣中，遂亡命到陳國。

translations.literal: His elder brother was killed by a townsman; Lang in broad daylight took a blade and took revenge inside the county seat, then fled his life to the state of Chen.
translations.idiomatic: He avenged his brother's murder in open daylight, then fled to Chen.

sentences.zh: 從博士卻仲信學春秋圖緯，[二]又詣太學受五經，京師長者李膺之徒爭從之。

translations.literal: He studied the Spring and Autumn and chart-weft texts with Doctor Xi Zhongxin, [2] also went to the Imperial Academy to receive the Five Classics; elders of the capital such as Li Ying vied to follow him.
translations.idiomatic: He read apocryphal Chunqiu with Xi Zhongxin, then mastered the Five Classics at the academy, where men like Li Ying flocked to him.

sentences.zh: 注[一]上虞，縣，故城在今越州余姚縣西。

translations.literal: Comment [1]: Shangyu is a county; its old walled city is west of Yuyao in Yuezhou today.
translations.idiomatic: Note [1]: old Shangyu lay west of modern Yuyao in Zhejiang.

sentences.zh: 有虞山，在縣東。

translations.literal: There is Mount Yu east of the county.
translations.idiomatic: Mount Yu stands east of the county seat.

sentences.zh: 注[二]孔子作春秋緯十二篇。

translations.literal: Comment [2]: Confucius made twelve weft treatises on the Spring and Autumn.
translations.idiomatic: Note [2]: the commentary attributes twelve Chunqiu weft texts to Confucius.

sentences.zh: 初辟司徒府，再遷彭城令。

translations.literal: At first he was summoned to the minister of education office, twice promoted to magistrate of Pengcheng.
translations.idiomatic: He entered the minister of education's bureau and twice rose to magistrate of Pengcheng.

sentences.zh: 時中官子弟為國相，多行非法，朗與更相章奏，幸臣忿疾，欲中之。

translations.literal: At the time sons of eunuchs were kingdom chancellors and mostly acted unlawfully; Lang with them exchanged memorial impeachments; the favored ministers were resentful and wished to strike him.
translations.idiomatic: He traded indictments with corrupt eunuch chancellors until the inner court plotted his ruin.

sentences.zh: [一]會九真賊起，乃共薦朗為九真都尉。

translations.literal: [1] When bandits arose in Jiuzhen, they jointly recommended Lang as commandant of Jiuzhen.
translations.idiomatic: [1] A rebellion in Jiuzhen became his enemies' pretext to banish him south as commandant.

sentences.zh: 到官，□厲吏兵，討破腢賊，斬首二千級。

translations.literal: When he reached office he drilled officers and soldiers, attacked and broke the Va bandits, and beheaded two thousand grades.
translations.idiomatic: He whipped the local army into shape and killed two thousand rebels.

sentences.zh: 桓帝美其功，征拜議郎。

translations.literal: Emperor Huan praised his merit and summoned him gentleman consultant.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Huan praised the campaign and recalled him as a consultant.

sentences.zh: 頃之，遷尚書。

translations.literal: Before long he was promoted minister.
translations.idiomatic: Soon he entered the ministry.

sentences.zh: 屢陳便宜，有所補益。

translations.literal: He repeatedly presented timely measures that brought supplement and benefit.
translations.idiomatic: He kept forwarding practical reforms from his desk.

sentences.zh: 出為河內太守，政稱三河表。

translations.literal: He went out as governor of Henei; his government was praised as a model of the Three Rivers.
translations.idiomatic: As governor of Henei he repeated his fame as a Three Rivers exemplar.

sentences.zh: 尚書令陳蕃薦朗公忠亮直，宜在機密，復征為尚書。

translations.literal: Minister Secretary Chen Fan recommended Lang as fair, loyal, bright, and straight, fit for the secretariat; he was again summoned minister.
translations.idiomatic: Chen Fan urged his recall as a blunt loyalist fit for the secretariat.

sentences.zh: 會被黨議，免歸家。

translations.literal: He met partisan impeachment and was dismissed to his home.
translations.idiomatic: The partisan proscription sent him home again.

sentences.zh: 注[一]中猶中傷。

translations.literal: Comment [1]: zhong means to wound with slander.
translations.idiomatic: Note [1]: zhong here means to frame or slander.

sentences.zh: 朗性矜嚴，閉門整法度，家人不見墯容。

translations.literal: Lang's nature was stern; he closed his gate and set the rules of the household so that even family members never saw a slack expression.
translations.idiomatic: At home he enforced such strict discipline that kin never saw him relax.

sentences.zh: 後竇武等誅，朗以黨被急征，行至牛渚，自殺。

translations.literal: After Dou Wu and the others were executed, Lang, because of the partisans, was urgently summoned; when he reached Niuzhu he killed himself.
translations.idiomatic: When Dou Wu died a warrant raced after him; he killed himself at Niuzhu.

sentences.zh: [一]著書數篇，號魏子雲。

translations.literal: [1] He wrote several treatises titled Master Wei.
translations.idiomatic: [1] He left essays collected under the title Master Wei.

sentences.zh: 注[一]牛渚，山名。

translations.literal: Comment [1]: Niuzhu is the name of a mountain.
translations.idiomatic: Note [1]: Niuzhu is a riverine mountain.

sentences.zh: 突出江中，謂為牛渚圻，在宣州當塗縣北也。

translations.literal: It juts into the Yangzi and is called the Niuzhu bend; it lies north of Dangtu in Xuancheng commandery today.
translations.idiomatic: The Niuzhu cliff north of modern Dangtu juts into the Yangzi.

sentences.zh: 夏馥字子治，陳留圉人也。

translations.literal: Xia Fu, courtesy name Zizhi, was a man of Yu in Chenliu.
translations.idiomatic: Xia Fu, style Zizhi, came from Yu in Chenliu.

sentences.zh: 少為書生，言行質直。

translations.literal: In youth he was a student; his words and deeds were plain and straight.
translations.idiomatic: As a young scholar he was blunt and plain-spoken.

sentences.zh: 同縣高氏﹑蔡氏並皆富殖，郡人畏而事之，唯馥比門不與交通，[一]由是為豪姓所仇。

translations.literal: The Gao and Cai families of the same county were both rich and powerful; the people of the commandery feared and served them—only Fu, though a next-door neighbor, would not deal with them, [1] and on that account was hated by the powerful surnames.
translations.idiomatic: He alone refused to curry favor with two rich clans next door, [1] and they hated him for it.

sentences.zh: 桓帝初，舉直言，不就。

translations.literal: At the beginning of Emperor Huan's reign he was recommended for outspoken counsel but did not go.
translations.idiomatic: Early in Emperor Huan's reign he was nominated for frank counsel yet stayed home.

sentences.zh: 注[一]比門猶並門也。

translations.literal: Comment [1]: bi men means sharing a neighboring gate.
translations.idiomatic: Note [1]: bi men means literally the next door over.

sentences.zh: 馥雖不交時宦，然以聲名為中官所憚，遂與范滂﹑張儉等俱被誣陷，詔下州郡，捕為黨魁。

translations.literal: Although Fu did not associate with the eunuchs of the time, because of his fame he was feared by the inner attendants; thereupon together with Fan Pang and Zhang Jian he was falsely accused, and an edict went down to the commanderies to capture him as a partisan chieftain.
translations.idiomatic: Though he never courted eunuchs, his reputation made them list him with Fan Pang and Zhang Jian as a ringleader.

sentences.zh: 及儉等亡命，經歷之處，皆被收考，辭所連引，布□天下。

translations.literal: When Jian and the others fled for their lives, wherever they passed people were seized and examined; the confessions linked and spread across the realm.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Jian's flight triggered arrests everywhere his path touched.

sentences.zh: 馥乃頓足而歎曰：「孽自己作，空污良善，一人逃死，禍及萬家，何以生為！

translations.literal: Fu thereupon stamped his foot and sighed, "The guilt arose from myself; in vain I would stain the good. One man flees death and calamity reaches ten thousand households—why should I go on living?"
translations.idiomatic: He cried that one fugitive doomed thousands and refused to prolong it.

sentences.zh: 」乃自翦須變形，入林慮山中，[一]隱匿姓名，為冶家傭。

translations.literal: " Thereupon he shaved his beard, changed his appearance, entered the Linyu mountains, [1] hid his name, and hired out to a foundry family.
translations.idiomatic: [1] He shaved his beard, fled to the Linyu hills, and hired on as a foundry laborer under a false name.

sentences.zh: 親突煙炭，形貌毀瘁，積二三年，人無知者。

translations.literal: He personally tended the smoke and charcoal; his looks were ruined and haggard; for two or three years no one knew him.
translations.idiomatic: Soot and sweat disfigured him until no one recognized him for years.

sentences.zh: 後馥弟靜，乘車馬，載縑帛，追之於涅陽市中。

translations.literal: Later Fu's younger brother Jing, riding in a carriage and cart, loaded with silk, pursued him to the market of Nieyang.
translations.idiomatic: His brother Jing tracked him to the Nieyang market with a cartload of silk.

sentences.zh: [二]遇馥不識，聞其言聲，乃覺而拜之。

translations.literal: [2] He met Fu but did not recognize him; hearing his voice he realized and bowed.
translations.idiomatic: [2] Jing failed to recognize him until he spoke, then fell to his knees.

sentences.zh: 馥避不與語，靜追隨至客舍，共宿。

translations.literal: Fu avoided speaking with him; Jing followed him to a guest inn and lodged together.
translations.idiomatic: Xia Fu would not answer; Jing followed him to an inn and stayed the night.

sentences.zh: 夜中密呼靜曰：「吾以守道疾惡，故為權宦所陷。

translations.literal: In the night he secretly called Jing, saying, "Because I kept the Way and hated evil I was trapped by powerful eunuchs.
translations.idiomatic: At midnight he whispered that his principles had brought the eunuchs' malice down on him.

sentences.zh: 且念營苟全，以庇性命，弟柰何載物相求，是以禍見追也。」

translations.literal: Moreover I thought only to scheme to preserve my life in full; why did you, my brother, load goods and come seeking me? That is why disaster followed.
translations.idiomatic: He scolded Jing for riding into a hiding place with treasure that could only draw the hunters.

sentences.zh: 明旦，別去。

translations.literal: At dawn they parted ways.
translations.idiomatic: They separated at first light.

sentences.zh: 黨禁未解而卒。

translations.literal: Before the partisan prohibition was lifted he died.
translations.idiomatic: He died before the ban on partisans was raised.

sentences.zh: 注[一]林慮，今相州縣。

translations.literal: Comment [1]: Linyu is a county in modern Xiangzhou.
translations.idiomatic: Note [1]: Linyu is in the area of modern Linzhou, Henan.

sentences.zh: 注[二]涅陽，縣，屬南陽郡。

translations.literal: Comment [2]: Nieyang is a county belonging to Nanyang commandery.
translations.idiomatic: Note [2]: Nieyang lay in Nanyang commandery.

sentences.zh: 宗慈字孝初，南陽安觿人也。

translations.literal: Zong Ci, courtesy name Xiaochu, was a man of Anxi in Nanyang.
translations.idiomatic: Zong Ci, style Xiaochu, came from Anxi in Nanyang.

sentences.zh: [一]舉孝廉，九辟公府，有道征，不就。

translations.literal: [1] Recommended filial and incorrupt, nine times summoned to ducal offices, summoned for possession of the Way—he did not go.
translations.idiomatic: [1] The court called him repeatedly for high office; he stayed home.

sentences.zh: 後為修武令。

translations.literal: Later he became magistrate of Xiuwu.
translations.idiomatic: He later served as magistrate of Xiuwu.

sentences.zh: 時太守出自權豪，多取貨賂，慈遂□官去。

translations.literal: At the time the governor came from a powerful faction and took many bribes; Ci thereupon left office.
translations.idiomatic: When a venal magnate became governor, Zong Ci quit.

sentences.zh: 征拜議郎，未到，道疾卒。

translations.literal: Summoned as gentleman consultant, before he arrived he died of illness on the road.
translations.idiomatic: He died en route to a consultant appointment.

sentences.zh: 南陽腢士皆重其義行。

translations.literal: Scholars of Nanyang all esteemed his righteous conduct.
translations.idiomatic: The gentry of Nanyang honored his integrity.

sentences.zh: 注[一]安觿在今南陽縣西南，仍有其名，無復基趾也。

translations.literal: Comment [1]: Anxi is southwest of present Nanyang county; the name survives but foundations are gone.
translations.idiomatic: Note [1]: old Anxi lay southwest of modern Nanyang city.

sentences.zh: 巴肅字恭祖，勃海高城人也。

translations.literal: Ba Su, courtesy name Gongzu, was a man of Gaocheng in Bohai.
translations.idiomatic: Ba Su, style Gongzu, came from Gaocheng in Bohai.

sentences.zh: [一]初察孝廉，歷慎令﹑貝丘長，[二]皆以郡守非其人，辭病去。

translations.literal: [1] At first examined filial and incorrupt, he served as magistrate of Shen and chief of Beiqiu, [2] in both cases because the governor was not the right man he pleaded illness and left.
translations.idiomatic: [1-2] He quit two posts rather than serve under unworthy governors.

sentences.zh: 辟公府，稍遷拜議郎。

translations.literal: Summoned to a ducal office, he was gradually promoted gentleman consultant.
translations.idiomatic: He entered a minister's bureau and rose to consultant.

sentences.zh: 與竇武﹑陳蕃等謀誅閹官，武等遇害，肅亦坐黨禁錮。

translations.literal: Together with Dou Wu and Chen Fan he plotted to execute the eunuchs; when Wu and the others were killed, Su also sat under partisan proscription.
translations.idiomatic: He joined Dou Wu's plot; when it failed he was proscribed as a partisan.

sentences.zh: 中常侍曹節後聞其謀，收之。

translations.literal: Later Regular Attendant Cao Jie heard of their plot and seized him.
translations.idiomatic: Cao Jie learned of the conspiracy and had him arrested.

sentences.zh: 肅自載詣縣，縣令見肅，入合解印綬與俱去。

translations.literal: Su drove himself to the county seat; the magistrate, seeing Su, entered the inner chamber, removed his seal and cord, and prepared to flee with him.
translations.idiomatic: The local magistrate tried to flee with Ba Su rather than arrest him.

sentences.zh: 肅曰：「為人臣者，有謀不敢隱，有罪不逃刑。

translations.literal: Su said, "As a minister, having a plan I dare not hide it; having guilt I dare not flee punishment.
translations.idiomatic: Ba Su said a loyal minister does not hide plots or flee justice.

sentences.zh: 既不隱其謀矣，又敢逃其刑乎？

translations.literal: Having not hidden the plan, how should I dare flee punishment?
translations.idiomatic: Having owned the plot, he refused to run.

sentences.zh: 」遂被害。

translations.literal: " Thereupon he was killed.
translations.idiomatic: He was executed.

sentences.zh: 刺史賈琮刊石立銘以記之。

translations.literal: Inspector Jia Cong carved a stone inscription to record it.
translations.idiomatic: Inspector Jia Cong set up a memorial stele.

sentences.zh: 注[一]高城，縣，故城在今滄州鹽山縣南。

translations.literal: Comment [1]: Gaocheng is a county; its old walled city lies south of Yanshan in Cangzhou today.
translations.idiomatic: Note [1]: old Gaocheng lay south of present Yanshan, Hebei.

sentences.zh: 注[二]慎，縣，屬汝南郡。

translations.literal: Comment [2]: Shen is a county belonging to Runan commandery.
translations.idiomatic: Note [2]: Shen county was in Runan.

sentences.zh: 貝丘，縣，屬清河郡。

translations.literal: Beiqiu is a county belonging to Qinghe commandery.
translations.idiomatic: Beiqiu county lay in Qinghe commandery.

sentences.zh: 范滂字孟博，汝南征羌人也。

translations.literal: Fan Pang, courtesy name Mengbo, was a man of Zhengqiang in Runan.
translations.idiomatic: Fan Pang, style Mengbo, came from Zhengqiang in Runan.

sentences.zh: [一]少厲清節，為州裡所服，舉孝廉﹑光祿四行。

translations.literal: [1] In youth he cultivated pure integrity, was esteemed by his district, and was recommended filial and incorrupt and for the four conduct categories of the chamberlain for ceremonies.
translations.idiomatic: [1] From youth he was known for austerity and won the top conduct recommendations.

sentences.zh: [二]時冀州饑荒，盜賊腢起，乃以滂為清詔使，案察之。

translations.literal: [2] At the time Ji Province suffered famine and bandits swelled; thereupon Pang was made envoy bearing a clarifying edict to investigate.
translations.idiomatic: [2] During Ji Province's famine he was sent as an imperial inspector with orders to clean house.

sentences.zh: 滂登車攬轡，慨然有澄清天下之志。

translations.literal: Pang mounted the carriage, took the reins, and was moved with resolve to clarify all under heaven.
translations.idiomatic: He mounted his chariot resolved to purge the province.

sentences.zh: 及至州境，守令自知臧污，望風解印綬去。

translations.literal: When he reached the province border, guardians knowing their corruption resigned their seals at rumor of his approach.
translations.idiomatic: Corrupt magistrates fled before he crossed the border.

sentences.zh: 其所舉奏，莫不厭塞觿議。

translations.literal: Whatever he raised in memorial satisfied the multitude's discussion.
translations.idiomatic: Every indictment he filed met public approval.

sentences.zh: 遷光祿勳主事。

translations.literal: He was promoted steward under the chamberlain for ceremonies.
translations.idiomatic: He became a steward in the chamberlain for ceremonies office.

sentences.zh: 時陳蕃為光祿勳，滂執公儀詣蕃，蕃不止之，滂懷恨，投版□官而去。

translations.literal: At the time Chen Fan was chamberlain for ceremonies; Pang observed full court etiquette in visiting Fan; Fan did not stop him from the ceremony; Pang harbored resentment, cast aside his tablet, and left office.
translations.idiomatic: Chen Fan received him with stiff formality; Fan Pang resigned in a huff.

sentences.zh: [三] 郭林宗聞而讓蕃曰：「若范孟博者，豈宜以公禮格之？

translations.literal: [3] Guo Tai heard and reproached Fan, saying, "For one like Fan Mengbo, should you fence him with public ceremony?"
translations.idiomatic: [3] Guo Tai scolded Chen Fan for treating Fan Pang like an ordinary subordinate.

sentences.zh: [四]今成其去就之名，得無自取不優之議也？

translations.literal: [4] Now you perfect his reputation for leaving—does that not invite criticism that you are less than magnanimous?
translations.idiomatic: [4] Guo Tai warned that Chen Fan had made himself look petty.

sentences.zh: 」蕃乃謝焉。

translations.literal: " Fan thereupon apologized.
translations.idiomatic: Chen Fan apologized.

sentences.zh: 注[一]征羌，解見來歙傳。

translations.literal: Comment [1]: Zhengqiang is explained in the biography of Lai Xi.
translations.idiomatic: Note [1]: see Lai Xi's biography for Zhengqiang.

sentences.zh: 謝承書曰：「汝南細陽人也。」

translations.literal: Xie Cheng's book says, "He was a man of Xiyang in Runan."
translations.idiomatic: Xie Cheng gives his native place as Xiyang instead.

sentences.zh: 注[二]漢官儀曰：「光祿舉敦厚﹑質樸﹑遜讓﹑節儉。

translations.literal: Comment [2]: the Han guan yi says, "The chamberlain for ceremonies recommends the four qualities: honest, plain, deferring, and frugal.
translations.idiomatic: Note [2]: the four conduct categories are defined in the Han guan yi.

sentences.zh: 」此為四行也。

translations.literal: " These are the four conduct categories.
translations.idiomatic: Those are the four virtues in question.

sentences.zh: 注[三]版，笏也。

translations.literal: Comment [3]: ban means the tablet (hu).
translations.idiomatic: Note [3]: the ban is the official tablet he threw down.

sentences.zh: 注[四]格，正也。

translations.literal: Comment [4]: ge means to correct or block with formality.
translations.idiomatic: Note [4]: ge here means to fence off with ceremony.

sentences.zh: 復為太尉黃瓊所辟。

translations.literal: Again he was summoned by Grand Commandant Huang Qiong.
translations.idiomatic: Grand Commandant Huang Qiong summoned him again.

sentences.zh: 後詔三府掾屬舉謠言，[一]滂奏刺史﹑二千石權豪之黨二十餘人。

translations.literal: Later an edict ordered stewards of the three offices to report hearsay complaints; [1] Pang memorialized more than twenty chancellors of two thousand piculs and powerful cliques.
translations.idiomatic: [1] In the hearsay-inquest round he impeached over twenty grandees and magnates.

sentences.zh: 尚書責滂所劾猥多，疑有私故。

translations.literal: The ministers secretary rebuked Pang for impeaching too many, suspecting private motives.
translations.idiomatic: The secretariat accused him of overreach and private grudges.

sentences.zh: 滂對曰：「臣之所舉，自非叨穢奸暴，深為民害，豈以污簡札哉！

translations.literal: Pang replied, "What your servant raises is never greedy filth or violent harm that deeply injures the people—would I stain the slips for that?"
translations.idiomatic: He swore he named only public predators, not private enemies.

sentences.zh: 閒以會日迫促，故先舉所急，其未審者，方更參實。

translations.literal: Only because the meeting day was rushed I first raised urgent matters; what is not yet verified I shall further investigate in substance.
translations.idiomatic: He asked time to verify the rest of his list.

sentences.zh: 臣聞農夫去草，嘉谷必茂；

translations.literal: Your servant has heard that when the farmer removes weeds the fine grain must flourish;
translations.idiomatic: He compared the purge to weeding a field.

sentences.zh: [二]忠臣除奸，王道以清。

translations.literal: [2] when the loyal minister removes wickedness the kingly way is thereby cleared.
translations.idiomatic: [2] Loyal ministers clear evil as farmers clear weeds.

sentences.zh: 若臣言有貳，甘受顯戮。」

translations.literal: If my words have duplicity I willingly accept manifest execution.
translations.idiomatic: He offered his life if any charge proved false.

sentences.zh: 吏不能詰。

translations.literal: The clerks could not refute him.
translations.idiomatic: The clerks had no reply.

sentences.zh: 滂鶯時方艱，知意不行，因投劾去。

translations.literal: Pang saw the time was difficult and his intent would not prevail; thereupon submitted a self-accusation and left.
translations.idiomatic: Seeing reform blocked, he resigned on principle.

sentences.zh: 注[一]漢官儀曰：「三公聽采長史臧否，人所疾苦，還條奏之，是為舉謠言也。

translations.literal: Comment [1]: the Han guan yi says, "The three dukes collect stewards' reports on good and evil, the people's hardships, and return them itemized in memorials—this is called reporting hearsay."
translations.idiomatic: Note [1]: the procedure required the three offices to channel popular complaints.

sentences.zh: 頃者舉謠言，掾屬令史都會殿上，主者大言，州郡行狀雲何，善者同聲稱之，不善者默爾銜枚。」

translations.literal: Recently for hearsay reports stewards and clerks all met in the hall; the presider spoke loudly asking the condition of provinces and commanderies; the good all praised in one voice, the bad kept silent as if with a bit in the mouth.
translations.idiomatic: The gloss describes the public inquest in the palace hall.

sentences.zh: 注[二]左傳曰：「為國家者，見惡如農夫之務去草焉。」

translations.literal: Comment [2]: the Zuo Commentary says, "Those who order a state see evil as the farmer must remove weeds."
translations.idiomatic: Note [2]: the Zuo compares governance to weeding.

sentences.zh: 太守宗資先聞其名，請署功曹，委任政事。

translations.literal: Governor Zong Zi had heard his name and asked him to serve as merit assessor, entrusting him with government affairs.
translations.idiomatic: Governor Zong Zi made him merit assessor and handed him real power.

sentences.zh: 滂在職，嚴整疾惡。

translations.literal: Pang in office was strict and hated evil.
translations.idiomatic: Fan Pang ran the office with iron integrity.

sentences.zh: 其有行違孝悌，不軌仁義者，皆埽多斥逐，不與共朝。

translations.literal: Whoever in conduct violated filial piety and fraternal duty or deviated from benevolence and right he swept away and expelled, not sharing court with them.
translations.idiomatic: He expelled anyone who flouted filial piety or humaneness.

sentences.zh: 顯薦異節，抽拔幽陋。

translations.literal: He clearly recommended men of unusual integrity and pulled up the hidden and obscure.
translations.idiomatic: He promoted hidden talent and shunned the corrupt.

sentences.zh: 滂外甥西平李頌，公族子孫，而為鄉曲所□，中常侍唐衡以頌請資，資用為吏。

translations.literal: Pang's sister's son Li Song from Xiping, a descendant of ducal houses, was maligned by local opinion; Regular Attendant Tang Heng asked Zi for Song's appointment; Zi employed him as a clerk.
translations.idiomatic: Governor Zong tried to slip in Fan Pang's unworthy nephew at a eunuch's request.

sentences.zh: 滂以非其人，寑而不召。

translations.literal: Pang thought him not the right man and laid the order aside without summoning him.
translations.idiomatic: Fan Pang shelved the appointment.

sentences.zh: 資遷怒，捶書佐朱零。

translations.literal: Zi shifted his anger and flogged scribal assistant Zhu Ling.
translations.idiomatic: Zong Zi beat his clerk Zhu Ling in rage.

sentences.zh: 零仰曰：「范滂清裁，猶以利刃齒腐朽。

translations.literal: Ling looked up and said, "Fan Pang's pure judgment is like using a sharp blade on rotten wood.
translations.idiomatic: Zhu Ling cried that Fan Pang's standards cut like a knife through rot.

sentences.zh: [一]今日寧受笞死，而滂不可違。

translations.literal: [1] Today I would rather be flogged to death than go against Pang.
translations.idiomatic: [1] Zhu Ling said he would die before countermanding Fan Pang.

sentences.zh: 」資乃止。

translations.literal: " Zi then stopped.
translations.idiomatic: Zong Zi relented.

sentences.zh: 郡中中人以下，莫不歸怨，乃指滂之所用以為「范黨」。

translations.literal: Those below the middling rank in the commandery all harbored resentment and pointed to those Pang employed as the "Fan faction."
translations.idiomatic: Petty men smeared his appointees as a Fan clique.

sentences.zh: 注[一]裁音才載反。

translations.literal: Comment [1]: for cai the reading is the cai-zai fan cut.
translations.idiomatic: Note [1]: fanqie gloss for cai in cai judgment.
