sentences.zh: 二二０四頁一二行滂鶯時方艱按：集解引王補說，謂袁紀「艱」下有「難」字。

translations.literal: Page 2204 line 12: Pang ying time was hard—note: collected commentary cites Wang supplement: Yuan Hong's annals add the graph nan after jian.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: add nan after jian per Yuan Hong.

sentences.zh: 二二０五頁一五行見則排斥按：刊誤謂「見則」案文當作「則見」。

translations.literal: Page 2205 line 15: when they see him they reject—note: Kan wu argues the text should read ze jian, not jian ze.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: word order jian ze versus ze jian.

sentences.zh: 二二０五頁一五行古之循善按：刊誤謂案文「循」當作「修」。

translations.literal: Page 2205 line 15: ancient xun shan—note: Kan wu argues xun should read xiu in the text.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: xun versus xiu for cultivate good.

sentences.zh: 二二０六頁一０行並衛侍於滂按：汲本、殿本「滂」作「傍」。

translations.literal: Page 2206 line 10: together wei shi at Pang—note: Ji and Palace editions write pang as bang (alongside).
translations.idiomatic: Collation: bang for pang in attend on Fan Pang.

sentences.zh: 二二０八頁三行周易曰鼓以動之殿本考證雲諸本同，王會汾謂案易無此文。

translations.literal: Page 2208 line 3: the Zhou Changes says the drum moves them—Palace edition investigation says all editions agree; Wang Huifen argues the Changes has no such sentence.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: disputed citation to Zhou yi.

sentences.zh: 張森楷校勘記謂「鼓」或當是「風」誤。

translations.literal: Zhang Senkai collation notes argues gu might be a miswriting of feng.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: gu versus feng in the gloss.

sentences.zh: 今按：注或引詩大序「風以動之」，展轉傳寫，誤「詩序」為「周易」，誤「風」為「鼓」耳。

translations.literal: Now note: the gloss may quote the Great Preface to the Odes "the wind moves them"; through repeated copying "preface to the Odes" became "Zhou Changes" and "wind" became "drum."
translations.idiomatic: Collation explains misattribution to Shijing preface.

sentences.zh: 二二０九頁五行*

translations.literal: Page 2209 line 5: graph*
translations.idiomatic: Collation: lacuna at 2209.5.

translations.literal: (zheng)
translations.idiomatic: Collation gloss zheng.

sentences.zh: **[復]*拜議郎據汲本、殿本改。

translations.literal: **[again]* appointed gentleman consultant—emended per Ji and Palace editions.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: supply fu again in appointment phrase.

sentences.zh: 按：前曾征拜議郎，故此雲復拜，作「征」誤。

translations.literal: Note: he had earlier been summoned gentleman consultant, therefore this says "again"; writing zheng is wrong.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: fu not zheng for again appointed.

sentences.zh: 二二０九頁六行家世冠族按：汲本、殿本「冠」上有「衣」字。

translations.literal: Page 2209 line 6: for generations a cap-and-gown clan—note: Ji and Palace editions add yi before guan.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: insert yi before guan clan.

sentences.zh: 二二０九頁八行司徒樊陵按：集解引錢大昕說，謂靈帝紀陵為太尉，非司徒。

translations.literal: Page 2209 line 8: Minister of Education Fan Ling—note: collected commentary cites Qian Daxin: Ling annals makes Ling grand commandant, not minister of education.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: Fan Ling's office title.

sentences.zh: 二二０九頁一０行司隸校尉許冰汲本、殿本「冰」作「永」。

translations.literal: Page 2209 line 10: Metropolitan Commandant Xu Bing—Ji and Palace editions write Bing as Yong.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: Xu Bing versus Xu Yong.

sentences.zh: 按：殿本考證謂「永」毛本作「冰」，監本作「水」，今從宋本。

translations.literal: Note: Palace edition investigation says Yong in Mao edition is Bing, Jian edition is shui—now follow Song edition.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: follow Song for Bing.

sentences.zh: 王先謙謂毛本並不作「冰」，不知所據何本。

translations.literal: Wang Xianqian says Mao editions do not write Bing at all—unclear which copy was used.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: Wang Xianqian doubts Mao Bing reading.

sentences.zh: 二二０九頁一０行幽州刺史楊熙按：「楊」原斗「揚」，逕改正。

translations.literal: Page 2209 line 10: Inspector of You Yang Xi—note: the original misprinted yang for Yang; corrected.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: surname Yang not yang raise.

sentences.zh: 二二一０頁五行由是結仇鄉人朱並汲本、殿本「結仇」下衍「覽等」二字。

translations.literal: Page 2210 line 5: on this account enmity was knotted townsman Zhu Bing—note: Ji and Palace editions wrongly add two graphs lan deng after jie chou.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: spurious lan deng after jie chou.

sentences.zh: 按：

translations.literal: Note:
translations.idiomatic: Collation note continues.

sentences.zh: 「覽等」二字如連上讀，當以「由是結仇覽等」句絕，然上文祗言侯覽與張儉結仇，不當有「等」字也。

translations.literal: If the two graphs lan deng attach to the preceding sentence, the break should be "on this account enmity was knotted with Lan and the rest"—yet the text above only says Hou Lan feuded with Zhang Jian, so there should be no deng.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: lan deng cannot end prior sentence.

sentences.zh: 如連下讀，則朱並成為侯覽之鄉人，通鑒即以「覽等」二字連下讀，而省去一「等」字，作「覽鄉人朱並」，然朱並為張儉之鄉人，非侯覽之鄉人也。

translations.literal: If they attach to the following, Zhu Bing becomes Hou Lan's townsman; the Comprehensive Mirror reads lan deng with the following and drops one deng as "Lan's townsman Zhu Bing"—yet Zhu Bing was Zhang Jian's townsman, not Hou Lan's.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: Zhu Bing cannot be Lan's townsman.

sentences.zh: 紹興本無此二字，乃知此二字為衍文。

translations.literal: The Shaoxing edition lacks these two graphs, showing they are interpolation.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: Shaoxing edition omits spurious graphs.

sentences.zh: 冊府元龜九四九正作「鄉人朱並告儉與同郡二十四人為黨」，亦一明證也。

translations.literal: The Cefu yuangui 949 correctly reads "townsman Zhu Bing reported Jian and twenty-four men of the same commandery as a faction"—another clear proof.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: Cefu yuangui confirms wording.

sentences.zh: 二二一０頁七行外黃令毛欽操兵到門按：外黃屬陳留郡，黃縣屬東萊郡，故顧炎武、錢大昕皆謂當作「黃令」，多一「外」字。

translations.literal: Page 2210 line 7: Magistrate of Waihuang Mao Qin bearing arms to the door—note: Waihuang belonged to Chenliu commandery, Huang county to Donglai; hence Gu Yanwu and Qian Daxin both say it should read "Magistrate of Huang," with an extra wai graph.
translations.idiomatic: Editors note that Waihuang lay in Chenliu while Huang county lay in Donglai; Gu Yanwu and Qian Daxin therefore argue the text should read magistrate of Huang, with the graph wai intruding by error.

sentences.zh: 惠棟則謂袁紀作「督郵毛欽」，或欽是外黃人，衍一「令」字耳。

translations.literal: Hui Dong says Yuan Hong's annals write "supervising secretary Mao Qin"—perhaps Qin was a man of Waihuang and ling is an interpolated graph.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: Mao Qin as supervising secretary variant.

sentences.zh: 二二一二頁二行其何傷於日月*[乎]*據汲本、殿本補，與論語合。

translations.literal: Page 2212 line 2: what harm to sun and moon hu—supplied per Ji and Palace editions to match the Analects.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: supply hu particle in Analects quote.

sentences.zh: 二二一二頁三行父*

translations.literal: Page 2212 line 3: father*
translations.idiomatic: Collation: lacuna in father's name.

translations.literal: (xiang)
translations.idiomatic: Collation gloss fragment.

sentences.zh: **[豫]*為南郡太守據汲本、殿本改。

translations.literal: **[Yu]* was governor of Nan commandery—emended per Ji and Palace editions.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: restore Yu graph for father's name.

sentences.zh: 按：殿本考證謂「豫」監本作「像」，從宋本改。

translations.literal: Note: Palace edition investigation says Yu in Jian edition was xiang; follow Song edition.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: Yu versus xiang in editions.

sentences.zh: 二二一二頁九行又以張牧為中賊曹吏按：刊誤謂案文多一「中」字，「吏」當作「史」。

translations.literal: Page 2212 line 9: also appointing Zhang Mu middle banditry bureau clerk—note: Kan wu argues the text has an extra zhong graph and li should read shi.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: zhong li versus banditry shi title.

sentences.zh: 二二一二頁一三行晊與牧亡匿齊魯之閒按：汲本、殿本「亡匿」上衍「遁逃」二字。

translations.literal: Page 2212 line 13: Zhi and Mu fled hiding between Qi and Lu—note: Ji and Palace editions wrongly add two graphs dun tao above wang ni.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: spurious dun tao before fled.

sentences.zh: 二二一三頁四行*[翔]*奏冀恃貴不敬據汲本、殿本補。

translations.literal: Page 2213 line 4: *[Xiang]* memorialized that Ji relied on nobility and was disrespectful—supplied per Ji and Palace editions.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: supply Xiang subject of memorial.

sentences.zh: 二二一三頁五行奏事中官按：校補謂案文「奏」當為「奉」之鬥。

translations.literal: Page 2213 line 5: submitting memorials through inner attendants—note: collation supplement argues zou should be a miswriting of feng.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: zou versus feng serve eunuchs.

sentences.zh: 又按：據張元濟後漢書校勘記，「官」原作「宮」，影印時描改為「官」。

translations.literal: Further note: according to Zhang Yuanji's collation notes on the Later Han, guan was originally gong, retouched to guan in the photolithograph.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: gong versus guan graph damage.

sentences.zh: 二二一四頁一行前書孔霸字次*

translations.literal: Page 2214 line 1: former book Kong Ba courtesy name ci*
translations.idiomatic: Collation: Kong Ba style name fragment.

translations.literal: (ru)
translations.idiomatic: Collation gloss ru.

sentences.zh: **[儒]*據汲本、殿本改，與前書合。

translations.literal: **[ru]*—emended per Ji and Palace editions to match the former book.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: ru character in Kong Ba name.

sentences.zh: 二二一四頁九行皆遽還之按：王先謙謂「遽」乃「追」之鬥。

translations.literal: Page 2214 line 9: all were hurriedly returned—note: Wang Xianqian says ju is a miswriting of zhui pursue.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: ju versus zhui for return land.

sentences.zh: 二二一五頁二行遠方至者常數百人按：「常」原作「嘗」，逕據汲本、殿本改。

translations.literal: Page 2215 line 2: those who came from afar were always several hundred—note: the original wrote chang as taste; corrected per Ji and Palace editions.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: chang always not taste.

sentences.zh: 二二一五頁七行□*[與]*子孫同衣而行據汲本、殿本補。

translations.literal: Page 2215 line 7: *[with]* sons and grandsons going out in the same clothes—supplied per Ji and Palace editions.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: supply with graph in poverty phrase.

sentences.zh: 二二一五頁一０行出為任城相按：「城」原斗「成」，逕據汲本、殿本改正。

translations.literal: Page 2215 line 10: sent out as chancellor of Rencheng—note: the original misprinted cheng for cheng wall; corrected per Ji and Palace editions.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: Rencheng city graph.

sentences.zh: 二二一五頁一二行令聞令望按：「聞」原作「問」，逕據汲本、殿本改。

translations.literal: Page 2215 line 12: fine fame fine expectation—note: the original wrote ask for wen; corrected per Ji and Palace editions.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: wen fame not ask.

sentences.zh: 二二一六頁三行而掾吏欲引南按：刊誤謂案文「吏」當作「史」。

translations.literal: Page 2216 line 3: but the clerks wished to lead him south—note: Kan wu argues li should read shi.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: li clerk versus shi scribe.

sentences.zh: 二二一七頁一０行亡匿汝南閒按：刊誤謂案文「閒」字下又云「有聲荊豫之域」，若只在汝南，則無用「閒」字，不當云「荊」，蓋漏「南郡」二字也，南郡則屬荊州。

translations.literal: Page 2217 line 10: fled hiding in Runan spaces—note: Kan wu argues after jian the text should include Nan commandery, since below it says reputation in Jing and Yu; Runan alone would not use jian nor warrant Jing; Nan commandery belongs to Jing province.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: supplement Nan commandery in flight phrase.

sentences.zh: 二二一八頁五行遣人西迎叔父爽按：刊誤謂案文致顒屍，又葬頤傍，則爽亦死矣，明脫一「喪」字。

translations.literal: Page 2218 line 5: sent men west to welcome his uncle Shuang—note: Kan wu argues since Yong's corpse was delivered and buried beside Yi's mound, Shuang was already dead too, so a sang mourning graph is missing.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: missing sang for Xun Shuang's death.
